metadata.source_paper: Satyana, A. H., Faulin, T., & Mulyati, S. N. (2011). Tectonic evolution of Sulawesi area: implications for proven and prospective petroleum plays. Proceedings of JCM Makassar 2011 — 36th HAGI & 40th IAGI Annual Convention, Makassar, 26–29 September 2011.
metadata.region: Sulawesi + surrounding region — full tectonic evolution
metadata.comprehension_summary: Full tectonic synthesis of Sulawesi from Oligocene to present. Sulawesi = collision between drifted Sundaland fragment (W) and Indian-Australian microcontinent fragments (E). Four arms = four megatectonic provinces. N arm = Paleogene-Neogene volcanic arc (subduction NST). E+SE arms = Mesozoic metamorphic+ophiolitic rocks from Oligocene obduction. S arm = Miocene volcanic/plutonic belt. Middle Miocene extension → Gorontalo Bay + Bone Gulf formation + core complex metamorphism in N Sulawesi. Late Miocene-Pliocene: Banggai-Sula + Buton-Tukang Besi collisions finalize 'K-shape'. Petroleum implications documented for all basins.
main_conclusions: Sulawesi formed by collision of drifted Sundaland fragments (W) with Indian-Australian microcontinents (E) during Oligocene-early Pliocene
Four megatectonic provinces corresponding to four arms: N arm (volcanic arc) + E+SE arms (metamorphic/ophiolitic) + S arm (magmatic belt)
Middle Miocene extension post-collision: formation of Gorontalo Bay (between N-E arms) and Bone Gulf (between S-SE arms) as deep pull-apart basins
Miocene core complex metamorphism in N Sulawesi during extensional phase
Banggai-Sula and Buton-Tukang Besi collisions: Late Miocene-early Pliocene, finalizing present K-shape
Extensional magmatism in S Sulawesi during Middle Miocene extension phase

properties.name: North Arm Volcanic Arc — Paleógeno-Neógeno
properties.full_name: North Arm Volcanic Arc — Late Paleogene to Neogene subduction-related arc, associated with North Sulawesi Trench subduction
properties.driving_subduction: North Sulawesi Trench (Celebes Sea subduction ~8-9 Ma)
properties.source_apa: Satyana et al. (2011). JCM Makassar 2011, HAGI-IAGI.
properties.paper_finding: North arm composed of late Paleogene to Neogene subduction-related volcanic arc, forming one of four megatectonic provinces of Sulawesi
properties.extraction_notes: Polígono del arco volcánico del brazo norte. Coincide con canon_nsarc existente; este feature aporta contexto temporal y de origen.

conclusions.text: North arm = subduction-related volcanic arc active since Late Paleogene; one of four megatectonic provinces defining Sulawesi K-shape
conclusions.geodyn_implication: El arco del brazo norte documenta el inicio de la subducción de la NST — vincula la formación del brazo norte con la geodinámica regional (inicio NST ~8-9 Ma según Greenfield 2021)

properties.name: South Arm Magmatic Belt — Mioceno
properties.full_name: South Arm Magmatic Belt — Miocene volcanic and plutonic rocks superimposed on older basement
properties.source_apa: Satyana et al. (2011). JCM Makassar 2011, HAGI-IAGI.
properties.paper_finding: South arm dominated by Miocene and younger volcanic and plutonic rocks forming a magmatic belt superimposed on older basement; related to Middle Miocene extensional phase
properties.extraction_notes: Polígono aproximado del brazo sur. Coincide aproximadamente con WSIP existente como province volcánica.

conclusions.text: South arm magmatic belt is Middle Miocene extensional magmatism — formed during post-collision extension that also opened Gorontalo Bay and Bone Gulf
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La extensión del Mioceno Medio en Sulawesi es un evento post-colisional que afecta simultáneamente el brazo sur (magmatismo) y las cuencas inter-brazo (Gorontalo, Bone) — sugiere colapso gravitacional post-orogénico

properties.name: Gorontalo Bay — pull-apart extensional Mioceno Medio
properties.full_name: Gorontalo Bay (Gulf of Tomini) — deep pull-apart basin, Middle Miocene extensional phase between N and E arms
properties.context: Formed during post-collision Middle Miocene extension between N arm (volcanic arc) and E arm (ophiolitic/metamorphic). Sister basin to Bone Gulf.
properties.source_apa: Satyana et al. (2011). JCM Makassar 2011, HAGI-IAGI.
properties.paper_finding: Gorontalo Bay formed during Middle Miocene extension as deep basin between N and E arms; extension accompanied by core complex metamorphism in N Sulawesi
properties.extraction_notes: Polígono del Golfo de Tomini (Gorontalo Bay). Coincide con canon_basin_gorontalo existente.

conclusions.text: Gorontalo Bay formed during Middle Miocene post-collision extension as deep pull-apart basin between N and E arms; contemporaneous with core complex metamorphism in N Sulawesi
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La extensión del Mioceno Medio en Sulawesi es un evento de escala litosférica que explica tanto las cuencas inter-brazo como el metamorfismo de core complex en el brazo norte

properties.name: Bone Gulf — pull-apart extensional Mioceno Medio
properties.full_name: Bone Gulf (Gulf of Bone) — deep pull-apart basin, Middle Miocene extensional phase between S and SE arms
properties.context: Sister basin to Gorontalo Bay; formed between S arm (magmatic belt) and SE arm (metamorphic/ophiolitic). Part of same Middle Miocene extensional event.
properties.source_apa: Satyana et al. (2011). JCM Makassar 2011, HAGI-IAGI.
properties.paper_finding: Bone Gulf formed during Middle Miocene extension as deep basin between S and SE arms; part of same extensional event as Gorontalo Bay
properties.extraction_notes: Polígono del Golfo de Bone. Coincide con canon_basin_bone existente.

conclusions.text: Bone Gulf and Gorontalo Bay are coeval Middle Miocene pull-apart basins formed by post-collision extension on opposite sides of Sulawesi
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La geometría simétrica de las cuencas (Gorontalo al N-NE, Bone al S) sugiere un mecanismo de extensión radial o colapso gravitacional centrado en el núcleo de Sulawesi durante el Mioceno Medio

properties.name: PMC — core complex metamorfismo Mioceno N Sulawesi
properties.full_name: Palu Metamorphic Complex (PMC) — Miocene core complex, N Sulawesi extensional phase
properties.context: Core complex metamorphism in N Sulawesi contemporaneous with Gorontalo Bay + Bone Gulf extension
properties.source_apa: Satyana et al. (2011). JCM Makassar 2011, HAGI-IAGI.
properties.paper_finding: Miocene core complex metamorphism in N Sulawesi during post-collision extensional phase; contemporaneous with Gorontalo Bay and Bone Gulf formation
properties.extraction_notes: El 'core complex metamorphism in north Sulawesi' del paper corresponde al PMC (Palu Metamorphic Complex) por posición geográfica. Polígono aproximado en área central-norte de Sulawesi.

conclusions.text: Miocene core complex metamorphism in N Sulawesi is coeval with Gorontalo Bay + Bone Gulf extensional basins — all part of Middle Miocene post-collision extension
conclusions.geodyn_implication: El PMC como core complex extensional del Mioceno Medio vincula la historia metamórfica con la apertura de las cuencas inter-brazo — extensión en profundidad (PMC) y en superficie (cuencas pull-apart) son simultáneas
